---
title: "MRIQC DB Aggregator"
subtitle: "BrainhackDC 5-minute group update"
author: "BrainhackDC working deck"
date: 2026-04-17
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "MRIQC DB Aggregator"
---

## Summary {.summary-slide .section-scale}

::: {.hero-grid}
::: {.hero-copy}
<div class="eyebrow">What we built</div>

<p class="lede"><strong>MRIQC DB Aggregator</strong> is a backend-plus-dashboard effort to make MRIQC DB easier to explore than the current page-oriented public API allows.</p>

<div class="tag-row">
  <span class="tag">representative sampling</span>
  <span class="tag">typed PostgreSQL warehouse</span>
  <span class="tag">dedupe-aware API</span>
  <span class="tag">working dashboard frontend</span>
</div>

<div class="callout">
  The core problem: the public MRIQC API is fine for record retrieval, but awkward for real-time analysis, filtering, and comparison.
</div>
:::

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="panel-title">This weekend’s outcome</div>

- pulled and archived representative raw MRIQC API payloads
- loaded them into PostgreSQL with explicit typing and dedupe metadata
- exposed dashboard-friendly read endpoints through FastAPI
- built a working frontend dashboard for metric comparison and dedupe-aware exploration

<div class="stat-grid">
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">231,100</span><span class="label">sampled observations</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">3</span><span class="label">initial modalities</span></div>
  <div class="stat"><span class="value">676 MB</span><span class="label">raw JSON archived</span></div>
</div>
:::
:::

## Backend {.section-scale}

::: {.grid-2 .design-equal}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Backend work</div>

- migrated against the application-level API contract, not Mongo internals
- stored exact pulled payloads first, then parsed them into typed tables
- kept `t1w`, `t2w`, and `bold` as separate fact tables
- used `source_api_id` for idempotent upserts
- preserved unknown fields in JSON extras instead of over-normalizing early

<div class="pill-list">
  <span class="pill">reproducible ingestion</span>
  <span class="pill">typed filters</span>
  <span class="pill">safe reruns</span>
  <span class="pill">forward-compatible schema</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Why this matters</div>

<div class="metric-list">
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">T1w exact duplicate groups</span><span class="number">3,047</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">T2w exact duplicate groups</span><span class="number">13,993</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">bold exact duplicate groups</span><span class="number">11,380</span></div>
  <div class="metric"><span class="name">API layer now serves</span><span class="number">overview, metrics, duplicates</span></div>
</div>

<div class="callout">
  We now have a storage and API shape that matches repeated dashboard reads much better than the original page-oriented service.
</div>
:::
:::

## Dashboard Design I

::: {.grid-2 .design-wide}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Design goals</div>

- start with overview plus drilldown
- keep dedupe mode visible: `raw`, `exact`, `series`
- make filtering simple and stable
- support a clear researcher question: “how does this slice compare to the broader MRIQC reference set?”

<div class="tag-row">
  <span class="tag">overview cards</span>
  <span class="tag">metric explorer</span>
  <span class="tag">duplicate inspection</span>
  <span class="tag">shareable filter state</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Design principle</div>

<div class="quote-block">
  Don’t make users think in API pages and raw documents. Let them think in modalities, filters, metrics, and comparisons.
</div>

<div class="timeline">
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Overview</div><div class="body">How much data is here, and what changes with dedupe mode?</div></div>
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Filter</div><div class="body">What happens if I slice by manufacturer, MRIQC version, date, or task?</div></div>
  <div class="timeline-item"><div class="phase">Inspect</div><div class="body">What does one metric distribution or duplicate pattern actually look like?</div></div>
</div>
:::
:::

## Dashboard Design II

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Wireframe direction</div>

```{=html}
<div class="mini-browser">
  <div class="toolbar">
    <span class="dot"></span><span class="dot"></span><span class="dot"></span>
    <code>/overview?view=series&amp;manufacturers=Siemens</code>
  </div>
  <div class="mock-dashboard">
    <div class="mock-sidebar">
      <div class="section-title"><strong>Filters</strong><span class="badge">sticky</span></div>
      <div class="filter-line"></div>
      <div class="filter-line mid"></div>
      <div class="filter-line short"></div>
      <div class="filter-line"></div>
      <div class="section-title"><strong>View mode</strong><span class="badge">raw / exact / series</span></div>
      <div class="filter-line mid"></div>
      <div class="filter-line short"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="mock-main">
      <div class="mock-panel">
        <div class="section-title"><strong>Archive summary</strong><span class="badge">fast aggregate</span></div>
        <div class="stat-grid">
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">7,222</span><span class="label">T1w exact</span></div>
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">60,670</span><span class="label">T2w exact</span></div>
          <div class="stat"><span class="value">45,710</span><span class="label">bold exact</span></div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="mock-row">
        <div class="mock-panel">
          <div class="section-title"><strong>Metric histogram</strong><span class="badge">same filters</span></div>
          <div class="chart-bars">
            <span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="mock-panel">
          <div class="section-title"><strong>Top values</strong><span class="badge">scanner / version</span></div>
          <div class="list-line"></div>
          <div class="list-line mid"></div>
          <div class="list-line short"></div>
          <div class="list-line"></div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Features we are designing toward</div>

- cohort-versus-reference comparisons
- saved views and shareable URLs
- smarter serving layers for hot queries
- clearer duplicate and missingness inspection

<div class="callout">
  The main design choice is to make the archive legible quickly, then let people drill down without losing context.
</div>
:::
:::

## Frontend Tech Stack

<div class="stack-grid">
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">UI</span><h3>React + TypeScript</h3><p>The current dashboard is a real React app with sidebar metric selection, dedupe-aware view switching, and a comparison grid.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">Build</span><h3>Vite</h3><p>Local iteration stays fast, and the dashboard can run in the compose stack alongside the API and database.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">API</span><h3>FastAPI</h3><p>The frontend reads from overview, metric summary, metric histogram, and duplicate endpoints exposed by the backend.</p></div>
  <div class="stack-card"><span class="stack-label">Storage</span><h3>PostgreSQL</h3><p>The dashboard works because the data now lives in typed, indexed tables instead of only behind paged Mongo-backed API reads.</p></div>
</div>

<div class="callout">
  The important integration story is simple: React frontend, FastAPI read layer, PostgreSQL-backed data model.
</div>

## Demo

::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Demo flow</div>

<div class="demo-steps">
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">1</div><div><strong>Start on overview.</strong> Show the high-level modality counts.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">2</div><div><strong>Toggle dedupe mode.</strong> Contrast `raw`, `exact`, and `series`.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">3</div><div><strong>Open one metric.</strong> Show that the same filters drive the histogram view.</div></div>
  <div class="demo-step"><div class="step-number">4</div><div><strong>Inspect duplicates.</strong> Explain why this matters before using the archive as a benchmark set.</div></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.card}
<div class="panel-title">Current status</div>

```text
GET /api/v1/overview
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/profile
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/metrics
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/metrics/{field_name}
GET /api/v1/modalities/{modality}/duplicates/{kind}
```

- backend demo path is real now
- frontend dashboard is real now and supports live metric comparison
- the live presentation site is part of the demo story too
:::
:::

## Future Directions

::: {.closing}
::: {.hero-copy}
<div class="eyebrow">What comes next</div>

<p class="lede"><strong>Next phase:</strong> tighten the dashboard around real researcher workflows and optimize the storage/read path only where usage proves it matters.</p>

<div class="roadmap">
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Frontend</h3><p>Turn the current dashboard direction into a more complete interactive interface.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Serving</h3><p>Add materialized views or pre-aggregates once the hot queries are obvious.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Comparisons</h3><p>Support cohort-versus-reference workflows more directly.</p></div>
  <div class="roadmap-item"><h3>Data model</h3><p>Normalize carefully around subjects, sessions, and scanners without inventing false universal keys.</p></div>
</div>
:::

::: {.hero-panel}
<div class="panel-title">Takeaway</div>

- we made MRIQC DB easier to query
- we made the ingestion path reproducible
- we made dedupe visible
- we created a clearer dashboard direction for continued work
:::
:::
